title: Jungle Mahal Zoological Park

overview.short: The Junglemahal Zoological Park (also informally called as Jhargram Zoological Park or Jhargram Mini Zoo) is a zoological park and a big tourist attraction in Jhargram, West Bengal
overview.description: The Junglemahal Zoological Park (also informally called as Jhargram Zoological Park or Jhargram Mini Zoo) is a zoological park and a big tourist attraction in Jhargram, West Bengal. Junglemahal Zoological Park is a recognized 'Medium' category Zoo by the Central Zoo Authority of India (CZAI). The CZA evaluates Zoos on periodic basis and stipulates conditions for their improvement.

topPlaces.description: Jhargram (Bengali pronunciation: [d͡ʒʱaɽɡram]) is a city and a municipality in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is the headquarters of the Jhargram district.

topPlaces.name: Dahijuri
topPlaces.description: Dahijuri is a village and a gram panchayat in the Binpur I CD block in the Jhargram subdivision of the Jhargram district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jungle Mahal Zoological Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jungle Mahal Zoological Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jungle Mahal Zoological Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (148 km) and the nearest railway station is Jamshedpur (Tatanagar) (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jungle Mahal Zoological Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jungle Mahal Zoological Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jungle Mahal Zoological Park?
faq.a: Stays in Jungle Mahal Zoological Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jungle Mahal Zoological Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jhargram (community development block), Jhargram, Dahijuri.
